subject: Accounting
title: Bank Reconciliation Step by Step
description: A quick, practical guide to Bank Reconciliation Step by Step.

hook.text: Your books say $23,450. The bank says $24,100. Both are correct—but they need reconciling.

buildup.text: Bank reconciliation matches your records against the bank statement to find and explain differences.

discovery.text: Common differences: outstanding checks, deposits in transit, bank fees, and interest earned.

twist.text: Sometimes the difference reveals an error—or worse, an unauthorized transaction you didn't know about.

climax.text: Monthly reconciliation catches problems early. Skipping it lets errors and fraud grow undetected.

punchline.text: Reconcile monthly. Surprises only get worse with time.

quiz.question: What causes differences between book and bank balances?
options: Outstanding checks, deposits in transit, and bank fees
